topic: Diagnose sk-design skill-routing faults and apply the sk-doc typed-pair routing optimizations. Investigate the per-mode INTENT_SIGNALS/RESOURCE_MAP config, how the skill-benchmark scores routing, whether generating a leaf-manifest plus typed gold would lift measured routing, and concrete optimizations. Produce findings and a resource-map.

normalized_topic: diagnose sk design skill routing faults and apply the sk doc typed pair routing optimizations investigate the per mode intent signals resource map config how the skill benchmark scores routing whether generating a leaf manifest plus typed gold would lift measured routing and concrete optimizations produce findings and a resource map

diff_summary: Appended bounded pre-init routing-research topic and canonical research context.
normalized_topic: diagnose sk design skill routing faults and apply the sk doc typed pair routing optimizations investigate the per mode intent signals resource map config how the skill benchmark scores routing whether generating a leaf manifest plus typed gold would lift measured routing and concrete optimizations produce findings and a resource map

focus: Classify each sk-design mode's router intent signals and resource leaves on the typed-pair surface, including the nested transport boundary.
noveltyJustification: All 5 findings are new in this first iteration and establish the six-mode typed-pair classification plus the transport boundary.
keyQuestions: How do the six sk-design modes map to independent (workflowMode, leafResourceId) gold pairs?
Can one byte-stable leaf manifest represent the parent hub and nested design-mcp-open-design transport?
answeredQuestions: How do the six sk-design modes map to independent (workflowMode, leafResourceId) gold pairs?
ruledOut: Treating hub entrypoints or shared defaults as a seventh workflowMode
Flattening design-mcp-open-design into a design-judgment mode
Using packet-qualified paths as leafResourceId values

focus: Leaf-manifest generator feasibility and byte stability for all six sk-design modes.
noveltyJustification: 4 of 5 findings were fully new and 1 confirmed a prior inference (raw 0.90), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for resolving the manifest-feasibility question.
keyQuestions: Can one byte-stable leaf manifest represent the parent hub and nested design-mcp-open-design transport?
answeredQuestions: Can one byte-stable leaf manifest represent the parent hub and nested design-mcp-open-design transport?
ruledOut: Excluding the transport because packetKind is transport
Treating absent leaf-aliases.json as a generation blocker
Using --check against a missing manifest as evidence of nondeterminism

focus: Scenario classification and independent typed-gold eligibility for the current playbook and frozen benchmark corpus.
noveltyJustification: All 5 findings are new and provide the first scenario-level typed-gold eligibility partition for both current and frozen corpora.
keyQuestions: Which benchmark scenarios are genuine routing decisions eligible for independently authored typed gold?
answeredQuestions: Which benchmark scenarios are genuine routing decisions eligible for independently authored typed gold?
ruledOut: Deriving gold from current router predictions
Typing advisor-negative scenarios as positive sk-design leaf routes
Assigning one aggregate pair set to multi-prompt batteries
Treating typed-pair correctness as full behavior or pipeline proof

focus: Fresh benchmark execution and per-dimension loss attribution for the approximately 69 CONDITIONAL baseline.
noveltyJustification: 3 of 5 findings were fully new and 2 were partially new (raw 0.80), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for resolving baseline attribution.
keyQuestions: Which scoring dimensions cause the approximately 69 CONDITIONAL baseline, and are they measurement gaps or routing faults?
answeredQuestions: Which scoring dimensions cause the approximately 69 CONDITIONAL baseline, and are they measurement gaps or routing faults?
ruledOut: Counting unscored D1-inter or D4 as zero-valued losses
Treating the fresh NO-SCENARIOS result as a routing score
Treating after_d3_proxy aggregate 100 as proof of routing improvement
sourcesQueried: command output: non-mutating in-memory run-skill-benchmark.cjs execution

focus: Dependency order, affected artifacts, acceptance checks, and rollback boundaries for a sibling implementation packet.
noveltyJustification: 1 of 5 findings is fully new and 4 are partially new syntheses (raw 0.60), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for one dependency graph with isolated rollback boundaries.
keyQuestions: What dependency-ordered optimizations can an implementation packet apply without more research?
answeredQuestions: What dependency-ordered optimizations can an implementation packet apply without more research?
ruledOut: Editing router intent or resource maps before a valid typed run proves a pair-level failure
Combining measurement enablement and router-quality edits in one change
Overwriting the frozen baseline or comparing different applicability shapes
Inventing shared aliases to make fixtures pass

focus: Stress-test nested transport attribution and dominant-mode narrowing for transport-only, design-only, and transport-plus-design scenarios.

focus: Freeze the implementation acceptance matrix and verify reproducibility/implementability without source mutation.
noveltyJustification: 1 of 5 findings is fully new, 2 are partially new, and 2 consolidate prior evidence (raw 0.40), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for one acceptance matrix and reduced verification ambiguity.
